conditional_states.condition: Form prefills from a record that may not yet exist or returns no data.
Multi-step or autosave form that can be saved or restored in an incomplete state.
Form submits over the network and must queue or warn when connectivity is lost.
Editing a record another user may have changed since it was loaded.
Concurrent edit detected on save (optimistic-lock / version mismatch).
User can view the form but lacks rights to submit or edit some fields.
Rendered on a coarse-pointer device requiring larger targets and native input affordances.
User prefers reduced motion; validation transitions must not rely on animation.

rationale: Forms are the primary write surface. Field-level focus, submission loading, blocking disabled state during submit, and clear inline error/success feedback are mandatory because data loss and ambiguous submission outcomes are the most damaging form failures. Keyboard operability is required, not optional, since forms are routinely completed without a mouse.
evidence: WCAG 2.2 3.3.1 (Error Identification) and 3.3.3 (Error Suggestion) require explicit, text-based error states.
Nielsen Norman Group: forms must show inline validation and disable the submit control while a request is in flight to prevent double submission.
Frappe/CRM and Helpdesk patterns: dirty-state tracking and conflict detection on shared records.
